case_title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. AMERICAN SURETY COMPANY

MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-21-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY JUDGMENT, DISCHARGE FORGEITURE AND EXONERATE BAIL PURSUANT TO PENAL CODE SECTION 1305.6(B)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AMERICAN SURETY COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY JUDGMENT, DISCHARGE FORFEITURE AND EXONERATE BAIL PURSUANT TO PENAL CODE 1305.6(B) IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT FILED THIS MOTION IN A TIMELY MANNER UNDER CCP 1013A(4) AND STATEN V. HEALE (1997) 57 CAL.APP.4TH 1084, 1087. THE ENVELOPE SHOWS A POSTMARK DATE OF 11/2/15, WHICH IS MORE THAN ONE DAY AFTER THE ALLEGED DEPOSIT DATE OF 10/23/15. THE COURT CAN THEREFORE FIND THAT THE DATE OF POSTAGE CANCELLATION (11/2/15) TO BE THE ACTUAL DATE OF SERVICE. DEFENDANT FILED THE INSTANT MOTION WITHIN 20-DAYS OF THAT DATE AND IT IS TIMELY UNDER PENAL CODE SEC. 1305.6(B). DEFENDANT MUST NEXT SHOW GOOD CAUSE FOR NOT MOVING FOR RELIEF WITHIN THE 185-DAY APPEARANCE PERIOD (WHICH EXPIRED ON 10/9/15). UNDER PEOPLE V. ACCREDITED (2014) 230 CAL.APP.4TH 548, 562, DEFENDANT MUST HAVE ACTED REASONABLY AND IN GOOD FAITH. HERE, THE HERRERA DECLARATION SHOWS THAT SHE THOUGHT THAT THE EXONERATION WOULD BE AUTOMATIC BECAUSE THAT IS HOW FLORIDA OPERATES AND SHE DID NOT KNOW THAT A MOTION WAS NECESSARY IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT'S MISUNDERSTANDING OF CALIFORNIA LAW AND ASSUMPTION THAT IT MIRRORS FLORIDA LAW IS NOT A REASONABLE GOOD CAUSE BASIS FOR RELIEF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
PAYMENT OF EXEMPT FEES FILE BY GOVERNMENT AGENCY

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgement
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diana Rosenstein of Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-3933 for Plaintiff.
John Rorabaugh telephone number 714-617-9600 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMERICAN SURETY COMPANY'S Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, Discharge Forfeiture and Exonerate Bail Pursuant to Penal Code 1305.6(b) is denied. The court finds that defendant filed this motion in a timely manner under CCP 1013a(4) and Staten v. Heale (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 1084, 1087. The envelope shows a postmark date of 11/2/15, which is more than one day after the alleged deposit date of 10/23/15. The court can therefore find that the date of postage cancellation (11/2/15) to be the actual date of service. Defendant filed the instant motion within 20-days of that date and it is timely under Penal Code sec. 1305.6(b). Defendant must next show good cause for not moving for relief within the 185-day appearance period (which expired on 10/9/15). Under People v. Accredited (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 548, 562, defendant must have acted reasonably and in good faith. Here, the Herrera declaration shows that she thought that the exoneration would be automatic because that is how Florida operates and she did not know that a motion was necessary in California. Defendant's misunderstanding of California law and assumption that it mirrors Florida law is not a reasonable good cause basis for relief. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
